title: Sound Engineering Technicians
description: Assemble and operate equipment to record, synchronize, mix, edit, or reproduce sound, including music, voices, or sound effects, for theater, video, film, television, podcasts, sporting events, and other productions.

tasks.text: Confer with producers, performers, and others to determine and achieve the desired sound for a production, such as a musical recording or a film.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Regulate volume level and sound quality during recording sessions, using control consoles.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Record speech, music, and other sounds on recording media, using recording equipment.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Separate instruments, vocals, and other sounds, and combine sounds during the mixing or postproduction stage.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up, test, and adjust recording equipment for recording sessions and live performances.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Report equipment problems and ensure that required repairs are made.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare for recording sessions by performing such activities as selecting and setting up microphones.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mix and edit voices, music, and taped sound effects for live performances and for prerecorded events, using sound mixing boards.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Tear down equipment after event completion.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Synchronize and equalize prerecorded dialogue, music, and sound effects with visual action of motion pictures or television productions, using control consoles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Reproduce and duplicate sound recordings from original recording media, using sound editing and duplication equipment.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Convert video and audio recordings into digital formats for editing or archiving.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Create musical instrument digital interface programs for music projects, commercials, or film postproduction.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
